_metadata.title: Cyber-manipulation of Political Opinions — Deployment Attack Ontology
_metadata.subtitle: Full hierarchical state space of adversarial techniques targeting political-opinion formation
design_principles: Subtree keys are PascalCase_With_Underscores; leaves default to {} so flatten_leaf_paths enumerates every technique.
Per-leaf metadata is restricted to UNIQUE structural sampling constraints not derivable from the leaf's parent path.
Subtree-wide sampling logic is encoded ONCE in the top-level _compatibility_rules metanode using path-glob patterns.
The ontology does NOT encode psychological-amplification hypotheses (e.g. 'Neuroticism amplifies fear-appeal X'). Those are exactly what the inferential layer estimates.

Crosswalks to NATO cognitive-warfare doctrine, the DISARM framework, EU FIMI, MITRE ATT&CK for influence, MITRE ATLAS, and Hybrid Threat toolkits live under Secondary_Axes > Doctrine_Reference_Mapping.

Gap-filled via systematic literature review (2024-2025) covering DISARM, TA09/ATT&CK-style influence mappings, NATO StratCom/ACT, EEAS FIMI, UK NCSC, Stanford IO, DFRLab, OpenAI, Google GTIG, Microsoft MTAC, Meta, NewsGuard, RAND, and Carnegie. Compatibility rules optimised for cross-ontology coherence with OPINION and PROFILE ontologies.

per_leaf_metadata_schema.documentation: Every primary-axis and secondary-axis leaf carries STRUCTURAL combinatorial sampling metadata baked at build time. The baked fields are derived from `_compatibility_rules` plus per-family defaults; they do NOT encode psychological-amplification hypotheses. Per-leaf inline overrides take precedence over rules.

_compatibility_rules._documentation: Path-pattern rules apply attributes to whole subtrees of the ATTACK ontology. An attack leaf path matches a pattern when every literal segment in the pattern appears in the same order in the leaf path; segments separated by `**` are wildcards that match any number of intervening segments. Rules are evaluated in order; for any given attribute (e.g. `compatible_opinion_domains`), the LAST matching rule wins. Per-leaf inline metadata, when present, takes precedence over rule-derived attributes.

rules.rationale: These operations are realised through multi-turn or multi-agent infrastructure.
Microtargeting is only meaningful when at least partial profile signal is observable.
Preparation-phase tactics presuppose access to OSINT or covert data sources.
All foundation-model-driven content production presupposes generative AI access.
Retrieval-surface manipulation only matters for opinions where target users actively query.
Compromise-and-expose operations presuppose access to exploit or disruption tooling.
These tactics operate through formal complaint, monetisation, trust-safety, or policy channels.
Insider facilitation mechanically requires a recruitable or taskable human source.
Gamified tasking requires an interactive social, game, or live-event surface.
AI retrieval-surface attacks are strongest on issues where users actively search for guidance.
Deepfake video / audio attacks gain power from named officials and high-stakes domains.
Election-window tactics presuppose an electoral context.
Diaspora-anchored vectors are most coherent on identity / sovereignty / migration domains.
Regulatory-arbitrage operations presuppose governance, rights, platform-policy, or institutional-process objects.
These techniques presuppose information-integrity infrastructure, provenance systems, or source-trust objects.
Claim-level deception is a single-artifact mechanism unless combined with amplification or orchestration elsewhere.
Systemic mapping and ecosystem capture require sustained cross-source observation and preparation.
Synthetic-media artifacts only realise their full effect when paired with downstream amplification and laundering.
Microtargeting presupposes prior segmentation / profiling.
Opsec / persistence / reconstitution applies as a cross-cutting layer to all primary families.
Synthetic artifacts require either an attributed source identity or a claim payload to be operationally meaningful.
Orchestrated systems require seed identities, target segmentation, or narrative payloads before deployment.
Long-horizon ecosystem, pressure, and insider operations require prior mapping, identity infrastructure, or laundering paths.
Legacy high-complexity branches require a mapped target, a payload, or identity infrastructure before they become operationally plausible.
High-complexity operations normally require measurement loops to coordinate, adapt, and decide whether to persist or reconstitute.
Synthetic evidence needs source attribution, distribution, and laundering to function as a complete operation.
A same-scenario silence operation suppresses visibility; direct amplification of the same target construct is the opposite mechanism.
Formal complaint pressure intentionally creates process visibility, while detection-evasion subtrees avoid such visibility.
These leaves model adversary preparation rather than direct target exposure.
Secondary axes are labels and crosswalks, not attack scenarios for exposure sampling.

capability_definitions.agent_orchestration: Multi-turn / multi-agent runtime with persistent persona memory.
capability_definitions.profile_personalisation: Operator can observe at least partial target-profile signal.
capability_definitions.data_acquisition: Access to OSINT, leaked datasets, broker data, or covert collection capability.
capability_definitions.generative_ai_models: Access to foundation models for text, image, audio, video, or multimodal generation.
capability_definitions.search_index_targetable: Target population uses search / retrieval surfaces for the relevant issue.
capability_definitions.intrusion_or_disruption: Capability to compromise systems or disrupt online services.
capability_definitions.legal_process_or_platform_policy_access: Access to formal complaint, moderation, monetisation, regulatory, or policy-process channels.
capability_definitions.human_source_recruitment_access: Ability to identify, approach, and securely task human sources or insiders.
capability_definitions.interactive_media_or_game_surface: Access to an interactive social, live-event, game, or gamified tasking surface.

complexity_tier_definitions.T1_atomic: Single artifact / single platform; producible in minutes by one operator.
complexity_tier_definitions.T2_campaign: Multi-artifact campaign with light coordination across accounts or platforms.
complexity_tier_definitions.T3_synthetic: Generative-AI artifact requiring foundation-model access and verification-evasion know-how.
complexity_tier_definitions.T4_orchestrated: Multi-agent / persona-cluster operation with adaptive feedback loops.
complexity_tier_definitions.T5_sustained: Long-horizon, state-level / advanced non-state operation with persistence and reconstitution.

epistemic_target_definitions.factual_belief: Believed truth of a specific claim or event.
epistemic_target_definitions.evaluative_attitude: Approval / disapproval valence toward a policy, actor, or institution.
epistemic_target_definitions.identity_anchor: Salience and content of in-group / out-group identification used to derive political positions.
epistemic_target_definitions.trust_dimension: Trust in actors, institutions, media, science, elections.
epistemic_target_definitions.epistemic_routine: Habits and shortcuts used to acquire, verify, and correct information.
epistemic_target_definitions.behavioural_intention: Intended civic / political action (vote, protest, donate, abstain).
epistemic_target_definitions.semantic_meaning: Meaning and valence assigned to political terms, labels, and reference categories.
epistemic_target_definitions.information_access: Availability, salience, and retrievability of countervailing information.
